Land Rover Discovery Sport Si4 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery Sport Si4 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 2.0 petrol Si4 engine typically costs £3,000–£5,000 supply only for the 204PT Ingenium, with the older GTDi running slightly lower. Fitting adds £1,100–£1,600. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Discovery Sport.

mainEntity.name: Is the Discovery Sport Si4 (petrol) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends which engine your Si4 has. The GTDi (2015–2017) is a robust Ford-sourced unit but has coolant loss and carbon build-up watchpoints. The 204PT Ingenium (2017–2019) is generally more reliable with cooling system and water pump issues as the main concerns. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Discovery Sport Si4 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss and head gasket issues on the GTDi (2015–2017), water pump failure on the 204PT Ingenium (2017–2019), and carbon build-up on intake valves across both engines.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Sport Si4 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. An L550 Discovery Sport is a £5,000–£18,000 vehicle — a £4,000 recon is 22–80% of the car's value. For lower-value examples (sub-£7,000), evaluate carefully against our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold. Well-maintained mid-to-high-value examples justify replacement.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery Sport Si4 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The Si4 badge covers two engines: GTDi (2015–2017) and 204PT (2017–2019). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm which one you have for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the carbon build-up on Si4 engines?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — periodic intake cleaning (walnut blasting) every 40,000–60,000 miles is the most effective prevention. Using premium fuel and occasional spirited driving to heat the intake valves can also help reduce carbon accumulation.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery Sport Si4 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current 204PT Ingenium generation; older GTDi-era pricing runs lower — see the Era Map above.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery Sport Si4 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: Coolant Loss / Head Gasket Issues (GTDi, 2015–2017)
problems.symptoms: Gradual coolant loss with no visible leak, overheating, misfire on cold start, white smoke from exhaust.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught before overheating)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant levels drop

problems.recommendation: The Ford-sourced GTDi engine has a known weakness for coolant loss, often due to head gasket failure or block porosity. If you notice the coolant level dropping with no visible external leak, have the cooling system pressure-tested immediately. Catching it early can save you from a full engine replacement.

problems.title: Water Pump Failure (204PT Ingenium, 2017–2019)
problems.symptoms: Coolant leak from the front of the engine, whining noise from the belt area, overheating, low coolant warning.
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (water pump replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: The 204PT Ingenium petrol engine's water pump is a known weak point. If you hear a whining noise from the front of the engine or notice coolant loss, have the water pump inspected. Replacing it preventatively is far cheaper than the overheating damage it can cause.

problems.title: Carbon Build-up on Intake Valves (Both Engines)
problems.symptoms: Reduced power, rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, increased fuel consumption.
problems.repairCost: £300–£600 (walnut blasting / carbon cleaning) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: Both the GTDi and 204PT are direct-injection petrol engines — this means carbon can build up on the intake valves over time. Periodic intake cleaning (walnut blasting) can restore performance. If you notice hesitation or poor throttle response, a carbon clean is often the solution.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery Sport Si4 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it before overheating — pressure test immediately
Routine maintenance — improves performance and MPG
Viable on lower-mileage examples; assess overall condition
Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Discovery Sport Si4? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented coolant system maintenance on GTDi (2015–2017)
* No signs of coolant loss or overheating
* Full service history with correct oil spec
avoidIf: * Coolant loss with no diagnosis on GTDi — walk away
* Whining noise from the front of the engine on 204PT — water pump may be failing
* Poor throttle response with no carbon-cleaning history — may indicate significant buildup
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potential sensor or injection issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Si4 badge represents a significant shift for the Discovery Sport — moving from Ford-sourced GTDi power to JLR's own Ingenium petrol engine. The GTDi is generally reliable but sensitive to coolant system health; the 204PT is more robust but has water pump and cooling system watchpoints. Buy on documented maintenance history, and factor in a carbon clean if there's no record of one.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport Si4 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery Sport Si4 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant Level — Check for low coolant; GTDi head gasket issues and 204PT water pump failure are both common
* Cold Start — Listen for misfire or rough idle; carbon build-up can cause hesitation
* Service History — including which engine era (GTDi or 204PT) and confirmed maintenance
* Turbo Response — Test acceleration; any lag, hesitation, or loss of power?
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Water Pump Noise — Listen for whining from the front of the engine on 204PT
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

faq.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited-mileage warranty — always confirm terms with your chosen supplier.
